responseMessageContents.text: The user is asking for a downloadable link like last time. Let me check the project history to find how the APK was shared before - likely uploaded to the Supabase storage bucket or some other method that was used previously. Let me check the existing APK file and previous methods.
Let me find how the APK was shared last time — there's an existing `ivx-holdings-v1.4.0.apk` in the project root already.

input.command:
# Check if there's an existing APK download method in the project
ls -la /home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.0.apk
echo "---"
# Check the old APK that was shared before
ls -la /home/user/rork-app/*.apk 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Check DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json for previous APK download URL
cat /home/user/rork-app/DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json 2>/dev/null | head -20
echo "---"
# Check if there's a download URL in the project history
grep -r "apk.*download\|download.*apk\|apk.*url\|apk.*link" /home/user/rork-app/DEPLOYMENT.md 2>/dev/null | head -5

output.stdout:
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user 82792662 Jul 14 04:49 /home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.0.apk
---
-rw-r--r-- 1 user user 82792662 Jul 14 04:49 /home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.0.apk
---
{
  "task": "IVX OWNER CHAT AUTH BUG — issuer_mismatch FIX",
  "timestamp": "2026-07-04T14:36:53Z",
  "rootCause": "Stale cached Supabase session on device — token iss claim from a previous build/project does not match the production Supabase project (kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb). The corrupted expo/.env also kept re-introducing ambiguity, and the preflight treated issuer_mismatch as a SOFT warning (passed the stale token to the backend, which rejected it → surfaced as the misleading 'No real owner session is signed in on this device (issuer_mismatch)' banner).",
  "fixApplied": {
    "frontendEnv": "expo/.env cleaned — only production values (api.ivxholding.com, kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co, valid anon key). Removed 127.0.0.1, localhost, pasted terminal label text, service_role key, junk GitHub/Render values.",
    "preflightHardening": "expo/src/modules/ivx-owner-ai/services/ownerSessionPreflight.ts — issuer_mismatch is now a HARD block (was soft). Returns an explicit OWNER_SESSION_REQUIRED result with the precise reason + names the Clear Stale Session recovery path. No more wasted round-trip to the backend with a guaranteed-rejected token.",
    "clearStaleSessionAction": "expo/src/modules/ivx-developer/authDiagnosticsService.ts — new clearStaleOwnerSession() function. Signs out + wipes the known Supabase AsyncStorage slots (sb-<ref>-auth-token, supabase.auth.token, legacy keys) so a stale token can never rehydrate. Next sign-in mints a fresh token from the production project.",
    "authDiagnosticsScreen": "expo/app/ivx/auth-diagnostics.tsx — added a primary 'Clear Stale Session (issuer_mismatch fix)' button in the one-tap recovery card + a 'Clear stale session' action button in the recovery actions row. Both call clearStaleOwnerSession() and re-run the diagnostic."
  },
  "filesChanged": [
    "expo/.env",
    "expo/src/modules/ivx-owner-ai/services/ownerSessionPreflight.ts",
    "expo/src/modules/ivx-developer/authDiagnosticsService.ts",
    "expo/app/ivx/auth-diagnostics.tsx"
  ],
  "headSha": "b836089bf0791e383f3be0be91ace8344fe2ca5a",
  "validation": {
    "typecheck": "PASSED — bun run typecheck, 0 errors",
    "runChecksExpo": "PASSED — Checks passed. No errors found."
---

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr:
{"command": "# Check if there's an existing APK download method in the project
ls -la /home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.0.apk
echo "---"
# Check the old APK that was shared before
ls -la /home/user/rork-app/*.apk 2>/dev/null
echo "---"
# Check DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json for previous APK download URL
cat /home/user/rork-app/DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json 2>/dev/null | head -20
echo "---"
# Check if there's a download URL in the project history
grep -r "apk.*download\|download.*apk\|apk.*url\|apk.*link" /home/user/rork-app/DEPLOYMENT.md 2>/dev/null | head -5"}

input.command:
# Check the project history for how APK was shared before
grep -r "\.apk" /home/user/rork-app/DEPLOYMENT.md 2>/dev/null | head -10
echo "---"
# Check for any previous upload scripts
grep -r "upload.*apk\|apk.*upload\|s3.*apk\|storage.*apk" /home/user/rork-app/ --include="*.mjs" --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" --include="*.sh" 2>/dev/null | head -10
echo "---"
# Check if Supabase storage was used for APK before
grep -r "supabase.*storage\|storage.*bucket\|s3.*upload" /home/user/rork-app/ --include="*.mjs" --include="*.ts" 2>/dev/null | head -10
